---
title: "qbData"
output: html_document
date: "2022-12-01"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(nflfastR)
library(dplyr, warn.conflicts = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
library(nflplotR)
library(ggrepel)

nflreadr::.clear_cache()
```

## File in which we analyze NFL QB's and their effectiveness.

```{r cars}
pbp <- load_pbp(2022) %>%
  filter(season_type == "REG") %>%
  filter(!is.na(posteam) & (pass == 1) & (!is.na(passer)))
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
epaPerPass <- pbp %>%
  group_by(passer) %>%
  mutate(totalAttempts = n(),
         avg_epa = mean(qb_epa),
         shortPass = mean(air_yards < 10, na.rm = T),
         mediumPass = mean(10 >= air_yards <= 25, na.rm = T),
         longPass = mean(air_yards > 25, na.rm = T)
         ) %>%
  summarize(passer,
            totalAttempts,
            avg_epa,
            shortPasses
            ) %>%
  filter(totalAttempts > 110) %>%
  unique()

ggplot(epaPerPass, aes(x = totalAttempts, y = avg_epa, label = passer)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_label_repel(
    min.segment.length = 0,
    max.overlaps = Inf,
    label.size = 0,
    label.padding = 0,
    label.r = 0,
    size = 4,
    family = "Monstercat"
  ) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = lm,
    se = FALSE,
    col = 'red',
    linetype = 'dashed'
  )
```
```{r}

```
Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
